summary: Task 18 (B1–B5) is implemented cleanly against the task brief. Core contracts — attachment decode on Persistent Room reads, cross-model reinit recovery via CancellationException→Idle + stale-Initializing release, default-model label, Draft→Persistent auto-resume, drawer footer — all land with appropriate guardrails (containment check, one-shot gate, cache, mutex discipline). Two substantive findings: auto-resume drops image/audio attachments silently on AC-R3 kill-resume (scope-acknowledged in comments but worth flagging against acceptance), and `observeFirstReadyThenResume` has a terminal-failure path that leaks the auto-resume across VM restarts without re-triggering. Plus a few minor items (cache eviction, Loading modelName during cold start, empty catch for attachment failure logging). No critical defects.

suggestions.suggestion: resumePendingAssistantPersistent(identity, userText) passes only the text of the unpaired USER row — attachments saved on that row (entity.imagePath / entity.audioPath) are NOT decoded and forwarded to the engine, so AC-R3 kill-mid-stream with an attached image/audio resumes as a text-only inference. The in-code comment acknowledges this as an MVP trade-off, but B4-AC1/AC2 do not explicitly carve it out, and B1 has just made the decode path available (decodeImageFromRelativePath / decodeAudioFromRelativePath already produce Attachment.Image / Attachment.Audio). This also diverges from user intent: the user's original prompt in the B4 scenario includes the attachment, and the model previously saw it before the kill.
suggestions.benefit: Align auto-resume with the actual USER-turn contents so the assistant's answer stays grounded in the attachment, closing a silent correctness gap on kill-resume-with-image.

suggestions.suggestion: observeFirstReadyThenResume suspends on `_uiState.first { it is ChatUiState.Ready }`. If the pinned engine transitions into `ChatUiState.Failed` (init failure, AC-U6 recovery path) the suspend never resumes — autoResumeAttempted stays false and autoResumeTarget stays set for the entire VM lifetime, but the auto-resume also never fires. After the user taps «Загрузить» and the engine reaches Ready, the first() collector finally proceeds and fires inference — that's actually the desired behaviour, but the subtlety is undocumented and relies on the VM instance surviving. More importantly, if the VM dies mid-wait (process kill → AC-R3), the next VM instance will re-derive autoResumeTarget from Room on bootstrap and try again, which is correct — but the current code doesn't express that invariant and a future refactor that caches `autoResumeAttempted` in SavedStateHandle would regress it.
suggestions.benefit: Add an explicit comment that Failed → stays armed, and that the one-shot gate is intentionally VM-scoped (NOT persisted across process death) so AC-R3 resume works. Optional: also collect _uiState.takeWhile { it !is Failed } and clear the auto-resume target on terminal Failed to avoid a stuck spinner semantic.

suggestions.suggestion: resumePendingAssistantPersistent sets `_uiState.value = ChatUiState.Ready(isGenerating = true)` BEFORE checking `currentReadyModel() ?: return`. Actually the order is correct (model check runs first, then state flip) — however if the model check fails, autoResumeAttempted has already been flipped to true by observeFirstReadyThenResume, autoResumeTarget has been cleared, and the message is permanently orphaned for this VM instance. The race (Ready → currentReadyModel → Idle in between) is rare because both run on Main, but a registry model-list re-publish between the `first { Ready }` wake and `currentReadyModel` read is theoretically possible.
suggestions.benefit: Either guard the auto-resume flag flip until dispatch actually starts, or document that a lost-resume-on-engine-flap is acceptable and user re-opens the chat to retry (new VM instance will re-derive the auto-resume).

suggestions.suggestion: attachmentCache is an unbounded `HashMap<Long, List<Attachment>>` that never evicts. Messages are only removed from `observeByChat` when the chat or row is deleted — the cache keeps references to their decoded Bitmaps indefinitely for the VM lifetime (VM dies on chat-screen pop, so bounded by one chat session). For chats with many historical images this can hold tens of MB of Bitmap memory. Task comment acknowledges LruCache as a follow-up; flagging for tracker-level visibility.
suggestions.benefit: Evict entries whose id is no longer in the emitted list (trivial: `attachmentCache.keys.retainAll(list.map { it.id }.toSet())` inside the `.map { ... }` stage), or migrate to `LruCache` with a sane max-entries. Keeps memory proportional to the visible message list rather than the full session history.

suggestions.suggestion: The StateFlow's `initialValue = TopAppBarState.Loading(modelId = "", modelName = null)` shows a generic spinner until the first `combine` emission. In `deriveTopAppBarState` when `warmupInFlight = true` and `effectiveModelId = ""` (very first emission before `_chatModelId` resolves), the same `modelName = null` path runs and the LoadingTitle falls back to `chat_topappbar_reloading`. B2-AC3 («Загружаю {modelName}…») is satisfied once the allowlist resolves, but during the initial ~1-2 frames the generic text flashes. Not a regression (B2-UX explicitly designs the fallback), but worth noting against `B2-AC3` if strictly interpreted.
suggestions.benefit: Either (a) document that the fallback is intentional cold-start behaviour, or (b) delay the Loading emission until `modelId` is known. The current behaviour is acceptable.

suggestions.suggestion: decodeAttachmentsForEntity captures both the SecurityException path (from resolveInsideAttachmentsRoot) and the file-missing / BitmapFactory-returns-null paths via `runCatching { decodeImageFromRelativePath(...) }.onFailure { errorLog.e(...) }`. But decodeImageFromRelativePath itself already logs the same two null cases internally (file missing, BitmapFactory null) — so a SecurityException is logged once (by runCatching.onFailure) while a missing file is logged once too, but the outer runCatching ALSO returns null for that case, which is fine. The more subtle issue: the outer `runCatching { ... }.onFailure { cause -> viewModelScope.launch { errorLog.e(...) } }` schedules the log write on `viewModelScope` but doesn't await — if the VM is cancelled immediately after decode failure, the log write is lost. Low-impact (attachment read failures are rare) but inconsistent with B1-AC2/AC3's 'errorLog.e called' wording if interpreted as synchronous.
suggestions.benefit: Either mark errorLog.e as fire-and-forget in the assertion, or use `errorLog.eBlocking` / await if errorLog exposes a suspend API. Current implementation is functionally correct; this is a test-observability note.

suggestions.suggestion: `buildMessagesFlow` comment claims 'Room dispatches `observeByChat` on its own background executor (not Main)' as justification for omitting `.flowOn(Dispatchers.IO)`. This is true for the real Room implementation but is an implementation detail of the DAO's dispatcher, not a contract of `Flow<List<MessageEntity>>`. A future DAO refactor that moves to `coroutineDispatcher = Main` (e.g. for a Paging3 migration) would silently push BitmapFactory decode to Main. The deterministic-test argument against `flowOn(Dispatchers.IO)` is valid, but the real-prod concern is load-bearing.
suggestions.benefit: Either add `.flowOn(Dispatchers.IO)` with a test-only wiring that swaps in `UnconfinedTestDispatcher` via an injected `ioDispatcher`, or tighten the comment to state that the DAO-dispatcher guarantee is a Phase-3 invariant that Phase 4 must re-verify. Cheap insurance against a subtle regression.

suggestions.suggestion: The release loop `filter { (it.initStatus === ModelInitStatus.Ready || it.initStatus === ModelInitStatus.Initializing) && it.model.name != modelName }` uses reference-equality `===` on `ModelInitStatus.Ready` / `Initializing`. Those are Kotlin `object` singletons so `===` is correct, but `ModelInitStatus.Failed` is a data class — the filter intentionally excludes Failed entries (a released engine should already be at Idle, and Failed entries have no native allocation). Worth adding a `// Failed intentionally excluded — native already cleaned up via err-path` comment so a future maintainer doesn't 'fix' the filter to include Failed and accidentally double-release.
suggestions.benefit: Prevent future regression where a well-meaning audit expands the filter and introduces a double-cleanUp on Failed models.

suggestions.suggestion: DrawerContent now has two similarly-named callbacks: `onNavigateToModelManager: (modelId: String) -> Unit` (existing, used by model-unavailable dialog) and `onOpenModelManager: () -> Unit` (new, used by footer). The call sites in SanctumApp.kt wire them to identical navigate-and-close-drawer behaviour. Keeping two callbacks avoids the nullable-modelId signature change the task doc listed as alternative, but the naming is ambiguous — a caller can't tell from the name which one opens 'the' model manager.
suggestions.benefit: Rename to e.g. `onOpenModelManagerForModel: (modelId: String) -> Unit` vs `onOpenModelManagerRoot: () -> Unit` for clarity, or consolidate to `onOpenModelManager: (modelId: String?) -> Unit` as the task notes suggested. Current form works but is a readability trap.

suggestions.suggestion: The new «Модель по умолчанию: {name}» label uses `Modifier.clickable { onOpenModelManager() }` on a plain `Text`. It lacks an explicit `Role.Button` semantic and `contentDescription`, so TalkBack reads it as static text. Consistency-wise, other clickable labels in the codebase set the role; this one doesn't.
suggestions.benefit: Add `Modifier.clickable(role = Role.Button, onClickLabel = stringResource(...))` so assistive tech can announce the label as actionable — minor a11y improvement.

suggestions.suggestion: `defaultModelName` uses `SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5_000L)` while neighbouring `hasDownloadedModels`/`activeModelName` use `Eagerly` / the registry-owned flow. The 5s grace window is fine, but the asymmetry means `defaultModelName.value` can be null for a HomeScreen re-entry even if `default_model_id` is populated, because WhileSubscribed will re-seed via the stateIn initial. Micro-correctness: the label will flash empty for one frame on re-entry.
suggestions.benefit: Align with the other HomeViewModel flows by using `Eagerly`, or accept the current flash and document it. Very minor.

suggestions.suggestion: wavToPcm reads `bitsPerSample` and `channels` from the header and uses them for duration math, but then `bytes.copyOfRange(44, 44 + dataSize)` assumes the canonical 44-byte header layout. A real WAV file with a 'LIST' / 'INFO' / 'fact' chunk between 'fmt ' and 'data' (common when ffmpeg produces the file) will have `dataSize` valid but the data offset will NOT be 44 — the function will slice from the wrong offset and return garbage PCM. Since the caller is always pcmToWav's inverse (the app's own writer) this is safe in practice, but the doc claims 'audio not written by pcmToWav (e.g. 8-bit payloads) still reports a truthful DecodedWav.durationMs' — that's only true for canonical 44-byte-header WAVs.
suggestions.benefit: Either tighten the doc to 'canonical 44-byte PCM/WAVE files only', or scan for the 'data' sub-chunk instead of hard-coding offset 44. The first fix is one-line.
